verified_fixes.evidence: lib/features/session/session_screen.dart:202-207 — HoldToTriggerButton.onHoldStart/onHoldRelease now call ref.read(sessionControllerProvider.notifier).holdStart()/holdRelease(); controller exposes holdStart/holdRelease proxies at lib/features/session/session_controller.dart:264,274 that forward to engine.holdStart/holdRelease

lib/main.dart:103-128 — _startBatteryAlertWatcher subscribes to batteryMonitorServiceProvider.onLowBattery BEFORE awaiting monitor.startMonitoring, and invokes sessionControllerProvider.notifier.startBatteryAlertSession on every fire

lib/features/session/session_controller.dart:386 passes onSimulationDescription:_appendFiredDescription; _appendFiredDescription at lines 542-554 copies into WalkSession.firedStepDescriptions; simulation_summary_screen.dart:22 reads session.firedStepDescriptions

lib/domain/models/step_config.dart:555 — constructor default this.callerName='Angela'; fromJson lines 572-575 uses json.containsKey('callerName') ? json['callerName'] as String? : 'Angela' so explicit null round-trips; copyWith (lines 628-656) has clearCallerName: true sentinel

lib/features/session/session_controller.dart:486-494 — auto-resume only when engineState is EnginePaused AND engineState.reason == PauseReason.incomingCall; userRequested/fakeCallAnswered/bootRestart pauses are preserved

lib/features/home/home_screen.dart:36-59 renders stealth.fakeName + stealth.fakeIcon; lib/features/session/session_screen.dart:101-110 hides branded AppBar title when stealth.enabled && stealth.sessionScreenStealth. stealth.notificationDisguise is documented as a composition-time hint (notification_service.dart:147-154). HOWEVER stealth.timerDisplay is set by stealth_screen.dart:62 but never read anywhere (SessionScreen always shows the timer via l.sessionRemaining). Noted as new bug below.

lib/core/utils/pin_hasher.dart — PHC-encoded argon2id with m=65536 KiB, t=3, p=4, 16-byte random salt, 32-byte digest via package:argon2; verify() uses _constantTimeEquals (lines 200-207). lib/core/widgets/pin_entry_dialog.dart:115,120 replaces plain == with PinHasher.verify. lib/features/settings/pin_setup_screen.dart:35 delegates to PinHasher.hash

lib/domain/models/session_context.dart:48,76 adds emergencyNumber field (defaults '112'); lib/features/session/session_controller.dart:360 seeds it from settings.emergencyCallNumber at _bootstrapSession; lib/domain/orchestration/strategies/call_emergency_strategy.dart:47-63 resolves in 3 tiers (step config -> eventDefaults -> context.emergencyNumber)

IncomingCallService.dispose (incoming_call_service.dart:82-89), NotificationService.dispose (notification_service.dart:283-291), HardwareButtonService.dispose (hardware_button_service.dart:117-122), GeofenceService.dispose (geofence_service.dart:102-106). All wired via ref.onDispose(service.dispose) in lib/services/service_providers.dart:149,165,183,220
lib/main.dart:63-72 — warms up appDatabaseProvider via customStatement('SELECT 1'); lines 75-80 awaits notificationServiceProvider.init(); both inside Sentry appRunner before runApp

verified_fixes.item: copyWith clear flags (SmsContact, CallEmergency, AppSettings PIN)
verified_fixes.evidence: SmsContactConfig.copyWith has clearContactIds (step_config.dart:833,844); CallEmergencyConfig.copyWith has clearEmergencyNumber (step_config.dart:1280,1286); AppSettings.copyWith has clearAppPinHash/clearSessionEndPinHash/clearDuressPinHash (app_settings.dart:146-168); SettingsController.setAppPinHash etc. use them (settings_controller.dart:63-90)

6 of 6 strategies that have real configs now use services.context.configFor(step) with try/catch StateError fallback: loud_alarm_strategy.dart:49, countdown_warning_strategy.dart:49, sms_contact_strategy.dart:108, fake_call_strategy.dart:48, call_emergency_strategy.dart:54, phone_call_contact_strategy.dart:128. disguised_reminder_strategy, hold_button_strategy, hardware_button_strategy are intentionally no-op / UI-driven and don't need configFor

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: TriggerManager declares batteryMonitorService as a required constructor parameter but still never invokes any method on it — the subscription now lives in main.dart's _startBatteryAlertWatcher instead, so the TM field is dead weight. Risk: callers assume setting up a TM is what wires battery; a future refactor could remove the main.dart watcher without any compile error in TM
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: final BatteryMonitorServiceProtocol batteryMonitorService; — `grep "batteryMonitor\." trigger_manager.dart` returns no call sites
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Drop the batteryMonitorService field + required constructor arg (and the call site at session_controller.dart:408) now that the top-level watcher owns battery. If it must stay for symmetry, log a clear "// intentionally unused — see main._startBatteryAlertWatcher" comment

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: AudioService still has no dispose() — the three lazy AudioPlayer instances and FlutterTts hold platform resources that are never released on hot-reload / provider teardown
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: AudioPlayer? _alarmPlayer; AudioPlayer? _ringtonePlayer; AudioPlayer? _voicePlayer; FlutterTts? _tts; — grep 'dispose' in audio_service.dart finds only stop(), never dispose()/shutdown()
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Add Future<void> dispose() that awaits _alarmPlayer?.dispose(), _ringtonePlayer?.dispose(), _voicePlayer?.dispose(), _tts?.stop() + _tts?.shutdown(); wire via ref.onDispose(service.dispose) on audioServiceProvider

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: NotificationService.showToast still creates a detached Timer(3s, _plugin.cancel(id)) that is NOT tracked in _scheduledTimers. dispose() only cancels _scheduledTimers, so every toast fired in the preceding 3 seconds keeps a live Timer that fires after dispose — platform callback against a closed plugin
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: Timer(const Duration(seconds: 3), () => _plugin.cancel(id: id)); — not inserted into _scheduledTimers, so dispose() loop at lines 284-287 never cancels it
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Store the toast timer: _scheduledTimers[id] = Timer(Duration(seconds:3), () { _scheduledTimers.remove(id); _plugin.cancel(id: id); });

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: AudioService TTS fallback phrase still hardcoded English
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: await _ttsEngine.speak('Hi, I am running late. I will call you back soon.');
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Accept the phrase via function parameter, resolved from localized AppLocalizations by caller (session context), rather than hardcoded

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: SmsContact / PhoneCallContact default templates still hardcoded English
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: const String _defaultSmsTemplate = '{name} may need help. Location: {location}. Time: {time}.'; — phone_call_contact_strategy.dart:40 has _defaultPreSmsTemplate equivalent
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Look up per-contact smsLanguageCode and pull default template from localized ARB

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: Simulation descriptions returned by all 9 strategies are hardcoded English ('[SIM] Loud alarm ...', '[SIM] Would call ...' etc.) and are rendered directly in SimulationSummaryScreen ListTile
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: return '[SIM] Loud alarm + $tail'; — same pattern in every simulationDescription()
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Return a symbolic (templateKey, Map<String,Object?> args) and resolve to l10n at the UI layer

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: FakeCallScreen still does NOT render FakeCallConfig.callerName — the screen shows only l.fakeCallTitle and has no reference to the current step's config
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: Text(l.fakeCallTitle, ...) — no FakeCallConfig/callerName reference anywhere in the 119-line file; FakeCallController exposes no caller-name accessor
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Expose the current fakeCall step's config via FakeCallController (read from SessionController's active step) and render callerName ?? 'Angela' in the overlay header

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: SessionController._fireDistressBecauseOfPin still accepts EndReason reason but ignores it — the wrongPinExhausted vs duressPin distinction never reaches the engine, so sessionEnded metadata loses the triggering reason
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: Future<void> _fireDistressBecauseOfPin(EndReason reason) async { ... runtime.engine.replaceWithDistressChain(await _currentDistressChainSteps()); } — reason is declared but not referenced in the body
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Either remove the unused parameter at both the call sites (line 303, 309, 313) or extend SessionEngine.replaceWithDistressChain to carry the EndReason so the sessionEnded event propagates it

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: SmsContactConfig.autoRecordAudio/autoRecordVideo/recordDurationSeconds/includeMedicalInfo/blackScreenMode still stored but never consumed by the strategy
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: executeReal only reads messageTemplate + contactSelection + channel + contactIds; the other fields are set in the UI editor but never acted on
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Either implement the audio/video/medical-info wiring or remove the UI toggles to avoid misleading users

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: PhoneCallContactConfig.preSmsIncludeLocation still ignored — the strategy calls resolvePlaceholders without supplying a location, so {location} is always empty regardless of the flag
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Plumb LocationService.getLastLocationUrl() through EventServices or SessionContext and pass as location: when config.preSmsIncludeLocation is true

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: DisguisedReminderConfig.intervalSeconds still not honoured — the strategy fires exactly one showDisguisedReminder per stepStarted; the interval is stored but ignored
previously_flagged_still_open.evidence: executeReal calls showDisguisedReminder once; intervalSeconds from the config is never consulted
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Wire intervalSeconds into scheduleNotification(delay:) or document that engine-level step retries drive repeats

previously_flagged_still_open.issue: SessionController._handleEngineEvent still `unawaited(orchestrator.handleEvent(event))` with no .onError handler — synchronous failures inside handleEvent are silently dropped
previously_flagged_still_open.suggested_fix: Wrap in .onError((e, s) => developer.log(...)) or rely on Zone.current.handleUncaughtError

new_findings.issue: PinHasher.hash and PinHasher.verify run Argon2id synchronously on the UI thread. At m=65536 KiB / t=3 / p=4 this is a multi-hundred-ms to >1s blocking operation on mid-range phones. Called from pin_setup_screen._submit (not awaited in an isolate) and from _PinDialogState._maybeSubmit on every digit once >=4 digits are entered (pin_entry_dialog.dart:107-127 calls PinHasher.verify twice for each digit beyond 4 — duress + sessionEnd — so every keystroke blocks the UI for 2x hash time)
new_findings.evidence: void _maybeSubmit() { final pin = _buffer.toString(); if (pin.length < 4) return; ... if (duress != null && PinHasher.verify(pin, duress)) { ... } ... if (sessionEnd != null && PinHasher.verify(pin, sessionEnd)) { ... } ... if (pin.length >= 8) { Navigator.of(context).pop(PinResult.wrong); } }
new_findings.suggested_fix: Move PinHasher.verify and PinHasher.hash to an isolate via `compute()` (or a worker isolate pool). Show a spinner while the future resolves; ignore further digit taps while the previous verify is pending. The current 'verify on every digit >=4' amplifies the cost 5x for an 8-digit duress PIN

new_findings.issue: PinSetupScreen._submit is fire-and-forget from _maybeAdvance — once _second.length >= _first.length, every subsequent digit tap re-triggers _submit() without waiting for the previous one. Each invocation runs PinHasher.hash (slow, synchronous on UI thread) and then awaits notifier.setAppPinHash(hash). Two concurrent _submit calls can both pop the page; concurrent setXxxPinHash calls race on the settings repository save
new_findings.evidence: if (_second.length >= _first.length) { _submit(); } — fire-and-forget inside a synchronous void _maybeAdvance; no concurrency guard
new_findings.suggested_fix: Add a bool _submitting guard set at the top of _submit; early-return in _maybeAdvance when already submitting. Also disable the keypad while _submitting is true to avoid keystroke leakage onto the next screen after pop

new_findings.issue: StealthConfig.timerDisplay is set by stealth_screen.dart:62 but never read anywhere in the session UI. SessionScreen always renders l.sessionRemaining unconditionally (session_screen.dart:184-188). The toggle appears functional in Settings but has zero runtime effect
new_findings.evidence: if (session.remainingSeconds != null) Text(l.sessionRemaining(session.remainingSeconds!), ...); — no reference to stealth.timerDisplay anywhere in session_screen.dart or home_screen.dart. `grep -r timerDisplay lib/` shows it set in stealth_screen and copied in StealthConfig model only
new_findings.suggested_fix: Gate the remaining-seconds Text on stealth.timerDisplay || !stealth.enabled || !stealth.sessionScreenStealth (or simply stealth.timerDisplay when stealth is active). Mirror in home_screen if there's a timer there too

new_findings.issue: AppDatabase._openConnection still uses direct `Platform.isAndroid` from dart:io instead of the PlatformInfo seam. Every other service (messaging, phone, device_state, system_ui, stealth_icon, notification) was refactored to use the injected _platform field; AppDatabase is the lone holdout. Harmless today but breaks the 'all Android branches behind PlatformInfo' invariant the audit established, and makes this line uncoverable from Linux test hosts
new_findings.suggested_fix: Accept a PlatformInfo in _openConnection (or pass via ctor) and branch on _platform.isAndroid. The workaround-open-sqlcipher call can stay conditional; tests can override with FakePlatformInfo(isAndroid: false) to skip it

open_questions: Should PinHasher.verify be moved into a worker isolate unconditionally, or should pin_entry_dialog batch verification (only verify when length plateau is reached, not on each digit from 4 to 8)? Current behavior runs 2-5 verifications per PIN prompt on the UI thread.
The FakeCallScreen still doesn't render callerName. Is this a deliberate omission (the step is mainly a sensory cue via ringtone/vibration) or an unclosed Phase 6 wiring gap?
Should TriggerManager drop the unused batteryMonitorService field now that main.dart owns the battery-alert subscription, or is it earmarked for a future in-session battery trigger?
